summary: Recent updates to Firefox's WebRTC support, which is the technology behind video calls and real-time communication, introduced tighter control over video quality by rejecting H.264 codec queries if the requested resolution or framerate exceeds set limits. This enhancement is important as it helps ensure that video streams remain within a manageable range, preventing overloading of devices during calls. Additionally, improvements were made to better handle media configurations and encoding information, ensuring that errors are properly communicated to developers. Overall, these changes enhance the browser's ability to deliver reliable, high-quality video experiences while maintaining system performance, thereby contributing to more efficient and effective online communication.
